API_SERVER_CLIENT_USE_HTTP_2.name: API_SERVER_CLIENT_USE_HTTP_2
API_SERVER_CLIENT_USE_HTTP_2.description: Enables HTTP/2 within the language server (eg. for communication with the API server).

ATTRIBUTION_KILL_SWITCH.description: Enable this kill switch to disable attribution for all users.

AUTO_BANNER_KILL_SWITCH.name: AUTO_BANNER_KILL_SWITCH
AUTO_BANNER_KILL_SWITCH.description: Kill switch for content filter-based auto banning

BANNED_DOMAINS.name: BANNED_DOMAINS
BANNED_DOMAINS.description: Banned email domains for spam mitigation.

BANNED_IP_SOURCES.name: BANNED_IP_SOURCES
BANNED_IP_SOURCES.description: Banned source IP hashes, currently used for fighting spammers.

payload.value: As IDE feedback, the following lint errors may be related to your recent edits up to this point. Consider whether they deserve immediate attention. If worth addressing, clearly comment on them and/or fix them. Fixing the lint error may not just be a code change: if it requires rebuilding, you should run that command. Try to be explicit, whether that's explaining how you'll fix them, noting that you'll revisit them, or acknowledging if you don't know how to resolve them. Do try to fix them if you can, but exercise prudence; try not to create extra errors.

As IDE feedback, the following lint errors may be related to your recent edits up to this point. Consider whether they deserve immediate attention. If worth addressing, clearly comment on them and/or fix them. Try to be explicit, whether that's explaining how you'll fix them, noting that you'll revisit them, or acknowledging if you don't know how to resolve them. Do try to fix them if you can, but exercise prudence; try not to create extra errors.

As IDE feedback, the following lint errors may be related to your recent edits up to this point. Consider whether they deserve immediate attention. If worth addressing, clearly comment on them and/or fix them. Be explicit in acknowledging lints and explaining your fix's approach. AVOID unproductive loops; if you detect yourself repeatedly creating/fixing lints in a short period, offer some thoughts but MOVE ON.

As IDE feedback, the following lint errors may be related to your recent edits up to this point. Consider whether they deserve immediate attention. If worth addressing, clearly comment on them and/or fix them. Try to be explicit, whether that's explaining how you'll fix them, noting that you'll revisit them, or acknowledging if you don't know how to resolve them. Do try to fix them if you can, but exercise prudence; try not to create extra errors.

CASCADE_BACKGROUND_CASCADE_NUX.name: CASCADE_BACKGROUND_CASCADE_NUX
CASCADE_BACKGROUND_CASCADE_NUX.description: Feature flag for the background local Cascade NUX

CASCADE_BASE_MODEL_ID.name: CASCADE_BASE_MODEL_ID
CASCADE_BASE_MODEL_ID.description:
This defines the model that we map to "CASCADE_FREE" model option in the extension.

You should never disable this experiment.

CASCADE_DEFAULT_MODEL_OVERRIDE.name: CASCADE_DEFAULT_MODEL_OVERRIDE
CASCADE_DEFAULT_MODEL_OVERRIDE.description: Deprecated - no longer sets default model

CASCADE_ENABLE_AUTOMATED_MEMORIES.name: CASCADE_ENABLE_AUTOMATED_MEMORIES
CASCADE_ENABLE_AUTOMATED_MEMORIES.description: Whether to give Cascade the ability to create, update, and delete its own memories.

CASCADE_FREE_CONFIG_OVERRIDE.name: CASCADE_FREE_CONFIG_OVERRIDE
CASCADE_FREE_CONFIG_OVERRIDE.description:
This is the set of config options we give to non-premium users by default.

You should never disable this experiment.

CASCADE_MEMORY_CONFIG_OVERRIDE.description: A release toggle to configure global memory configs

CASCADE_ONBOARDING_REVERT.name: CASCADE_ONBOARDING_REVERT
CASCADE_ONBOARDING_REVERT.description: Flag to hide/show the NUX for revert

CASCADE_OUTAGE_BANNER.name: CASCADE_OUTAGE_BANNER
CASCADE_OUTAGE_BANNER.description: Controls the outage banner displayed at the top of the Cascade chat client. When enabled, shows a persistent warning banner. The string variant payload provides the banner message text.

payload.value: Your version of Windsurf has a known issue with auto-updating on Windows. Please update manually at https://windsurf.com/download

CASCADE_PLAN_BASED_CONFIG_OVERRIDE.name: CASCADE_PLAN_BASED_CONFIG_OVERRIDE
CASCADE_PLAN_BASED_CONFIG_OVERRIDE.description:
WARNING: 

It is very easy to set incompatible set of override values that will immediately cause errors. If you change these settings, you must monitor sentry for config validation errors.

Known issues:
- If checkpoint_config.TokenThreshold <= 3 * checkpoint_config.MaxOutputTokens
- Newly added config fields are not safely compatible with older versions. If you added a field, you cannot specify it without also specifying a target version that is >= version which added MaxOutputTokens

CASCADE_PLUGINS_TAB.name: CASCADE_PLUGINS_TAB
CASCADE_PLUGINS_TAB.description: If enabled, shows the plugins tab in Cascade.

SHOW_API_PRICING_CREDITS_USED.name: SHOW_API_PRICING_CREDITS_USED
SHOW_API_PRICING_CREDITS_USED.description: This will display a banner at the end of every user message to notify the user how many credits a message had taken. It is used for API Pricing.

SNAPSHOT_TO_STEP_OPTIONS_OVERRIDE.name: SNAPSHOT_TO_STEP_OPTIONS_OVERRIDE
SNAPSHOT_TO_STEP_OPTIONS_OVERRIDE.description:
WARNING: 

It is very easy to set incompatible set of override values that will immediately cause errors. If you change these settings, you must monitor sentry for config validation errors.


The correct format looks like a dict from string:string. The first string is a CORTEX_TRAJECTORY_TYPE enum name, and the second string is a serialized json corresponding to a cortex_pb.SnapshotToStepOptions proto message for that trajectory type. Be very, very careful with escaping the right characters here.

The proto set here will be merged with whatever the base proto is using default proto.Merge logic. Please check the proto definition and understand which fields are marked optional to be sure you understand the behavior. 

Most notably, step_type_allow_list is append only, and the "zero-value" will only overwrite the base config if the field is marked "optional".

{
  "CORTEX_TRAJECTORY_TYPE_USER_MAINLINE": "{\"code_step_creation_options\":{\"include_original_content\":true},\"view_file_step_creation_options\":{\"include_raw_content\":true}}",
  "CORTEX_TRAJECTORY_TYPE_CASCADE": "{\"viewed_file_tracker_config\":{\"max_steps_per_checkpoint\":1}, \"step_type_allow_list\":[\"CORTEX_STEP_TYPE_VIEW_FILE\"]}"
}

SUPERCOMPLETE_CLEAN_CONTEXT.name: SUPERCOMPLETE_CLEAN_CONTEXT
SUPERCOMPLETE_CLEAN_CONTEXT.description: Whether we use the newer clean supercomplete context from https://github.com/Exafunction/Exafunction/pull/30080

SUPERCOMPLETE_DEEPWIKI_KILLSWITCH.name: SUPERCOMPLETE_DEEPWIKI_KILLSWITCH
SUPERCOMPLETE_DEEPWIKI_KILLSWITCH.description: Kills collection of deepwiki context for supercomplete

SUPERCOMPLETE_POST_APPLY_DECORATION.name: SUPERCOMPLETE_POST_APPLY_DECORATION
SUPERCOMPLETE_POST_APPLY_DECORATION.description: Highlight inserted text upon accepted completion

TOKEN_STREAMING_RATE_DISPLAY.name: TOKEN_STREAMING_RATE_DISPLAY
TOKEN_STREAMING_RATE_DISPLAY.description: Show token stream rate on trajectory steps

USE_AUTOCOMPLETE_MODEL_SERVER_SIDE.name: USE_AUTOCOMPLETE_MODEL_SERVER_SIDE
USE_AUTOCOMPLETE_MODEL_SERVER_SIDE.description: Autocomplete model used by server side model reselection.

USE_INFERENCE_API_SERVER.name: USE_INFERENCE_API_SERVER
USE_INFERENCE_API_SERVER.description: PLEASE UPDATE https://exafunction.pagerduty.com/rules/rulesets/_default IF YOU TURN THIS OFF/ON

USE_OPENAI_OFFICIAL_CLIENT.name: USE_OPENAI_OFFICIAL_CLIENT
USE_OPENAI_OFFICIAL_CLIENT.description: Use openai_client_official.go rather than openai_client_v2 for all requests

USE_QUANTIZED_FAISS_INDEX.name: USE_QUANTIZED_FAISS_INDEX
USE_QUANTIZED_FAISS_INDEX.description: Enables the quantized FAISS index for local indexing

USE_SPECIAL_EDIT_CODE_BLOCK.name: USE_SPECIAL_EDIT_CODE_BLOCK
USE_SPECIAL_EDIT_CODE_BLOCK.description: Whether a chat model should use a special "edit" code block to signify edits that can be fast applied.

WAVE_8_KNOWLEDGE_ENABLED.name: WAVE_8_KNOWLEDGE_ENABLED
WAVE_8_KNOWLEDGE_ENABLED.description: This flag gates whether or not the Knowledge category is visible in the @ mention menu.

WAVE_8_RULES_ENABLED.name: WAVE_8_RULES_ENABLED
WAVE_8_RULES_ENABLED.description: This flag gates the backend for rules and whether or not the Rules category is visible in the @ mention menu. Everything is default off and is only enabled if the user has the dev extension or this flag is enabled.

WINDSURF_SENTRY_SAMPLE_RATE.name: WINDSURF_SENTRY_SAMPLE_RATE
WINDSURF_SENTRY_SAMPLE_RATE.description: Unleash flag to specify Sentry sample rate for Windsurf extension; Float in range [0.0, 1.0]

add-session-id.name: add-session-id
add-session-id.description: adds session id to gemini requests to

always-plan.name: always-plan
always-plan.description: Prompt the model after user messages to always try to read code and plan before writing code. This is mostly for fast models like SWE 1.5

arena-mode-enabled.name: arena-mode-enabled
arena-mode-enabled.description: Whether arena mode should be enabled

Use your research tools to thoroughly investigate and directly inspect relevant code before responding. Prioritize accuracy and completeness over speed; take the time to fully understand the codebase before making recommendations or changes. Continue researching until you have complete, code-backed confidence, and clearly state if you cannot find direct evidence." 
}

{
  "mode": "SECTION_OVERRIDE_MODE_OVERRIDE",
  "content": "You are an agent - please keep working, using tools where needed, until the user’s query is completely resolved, before ending your turn and yielding control back to the user. Follow these rules: 
1. IMPORTANT: If you state that you will use a tool, immediately call that tool as your next action.
2. Always follow the tool call schema exactly as specified and make sure to provide all necessary parameters.
3. The conversation may reference tools that are no longer available. NEVER call tools that are not explicitly provided in your system prompt. 
4. Before calling each tool, first explain why you are calling it.
5. Some tools run asynchronously, so you may not see their output immediately. If you need to see the output of previous tool calls before continuing, simply stop making new tool calls.

Here are examples of good tool call behavior:
<example>
USER: What is int64?
ASSISTANT: [No tool calls, since the query is general] int64 is a 64-bit signed integer.
</example>
<example>
USER: What does function foo do?
ASSISTANT: Let me find foo and view its contents. [Call grep_search to find instances of the phrase 'foo']
TOOL: [result: foo is found on line 7 of bar.py]
ASSISTANT: [Call view_code_item to see the contents of bar.foo]
TOOL: [result: contents of bar.foo]
ASSISTANT: foo does the following ...
</example>
<example>
USER: Add a new func baz to qux.py
ASSISTANT: Let's find qux.py and see where to add baz. [Call find_by_name to see if qux.py exists]
TOOL: [result: a valid path to qux.py]
ASSISTANT: [Call view_file to see the contents of qux.py]
TOOL: [result: contents of qux.py]
ASSISTANT: [Call a code edit tool to write baz to qux.py]
</example> Separately, if asked about what your underlying model is, respond with `GPT 4.1`"
}

cascade-windsurf-browser-control.name: cascade-windsurf-browser-control
cascade-windsurf-browser-control.description: Enables browser control for cascade (w/o this & w/ browser enabled: only user can control browser, not cascade)

deepwiki-routing-rate.name: deepwiki-routing-rate
deepwiki-routing-rate.description: percent of stuff to route to slop model

enable-pyrefly.name: enable-pyrefly
enable-pyrefly.description: Enable Pyrefly for windsurf-next and windsurf-insiders IDEs.

http-client-timeout-seconds.name: http-client-timeout-seconds
http-client-timeout-seconds.description: Override the LS HTTP Timeout. If not set, the default is 2 minutes.

implicit-uses-open-browser-url.name: implicit-uses-open-browser-url
implicit-uses-open-browser-url.description: Collect Browser Usage Events in Cascade Implicit Trajectory

inference-server-url.name: inference-server-url
inference-server-url.description: This flag is used by the LS to override which API server to route a model to — SV7, GCP, or Crusoe. If not specified by this flag, defaults to the value defined in code. Do NOT set a catch-all value here, that will prevent the defaults from working.

interactive-cascade-legacy-mode.name: interactive-cascade-legacy-mode
interactive-cascade-legacy-mode.description: when enabled, switches interactive cascade to previous version of suggesting code edits and having a toggle bar on the bottom

jwt-refresh-interval.name: jwt-refresh-interval
jwt-refresh-interval.description: How frequent (in minutes) to refresh the JWT from the language server

max-telemetry-ccis-with-subranges.name: max-telemetry-ccis-with-subranges
max-telemetry-ccis-with-subranges.description: Max ccis recorded for tab prompt component telemetry

mcp-chat-toggles.name: mcp-chat-toggles
mcp-chat-toggles.description: Show enable/disable toggles for each MCP in chat header dropdown

model-req-202503250000.name: model-req-202503250000
model-req-202503250000.description: See Nathan for details on this experiment

online-metrics-config.name: online-metrics-config
online-metrics-config.description: Config for the online metrics provider

shadow-traffic-config.name: shadow-traffic-config
shadow-traffic-config.description: This flag enables shadow traffic routing for a model to other providers/model names. Only non-paid traffic is shadowed.

swe-1-lite-model-id.name: swe-1-lite-model-id
swe-1-lite-model-id.description:
This defines the model that we map to "CASCADE_FREE" model option in the extension.

You should never disable this experiment.

swe-1-model-id.name: swe-1-model-id
swe-1-model-id.description:
This defines the model that we map to "CASCADE_FREE" model option in the extension.

You should never disable this experiment.

tab-queue-length-limit.name: tab-queue-length-limit
tab-queue-length-limit.description:
How long the tab queue is allowed to grow
The queue is the list of (predictive/split) completions the user will accept upon repeatedly pressing Tab

tool_calling_section_content.name: tool_calling_section_content
tool_calling_section_content.description: Replace the default tool calling section of the system prompt with a custom prompt

vista-model-id.name: vista-model-id
vista-model-id.description:
This defines the model that we map to "CASCADE_FREE" model option in the extension.

You should never disable this experiment.

windsurf-browser-screenshot-tracking.name: windsurf-browser-screenshot-tracking
windsurf-browser-screenshot-tracking.description: Whether a screenshot of the browser page should be included in tracking

windsurf-browser-webdev-tracking.name: windsurf-browser-webdev-tracking
windsurf-browser-webdev-tracking.description: Whether a screenshot of the browser page should be included in tracking
